sentences: Anna never thought her worst day would start with a call.
It was Tuesday morning when she was at work and her phone rang.
The voice was calm but felt urgent.
There was an accident.
Anna's son Ethan is eight years old and goes to the hospital.
Anna's world stops when she feels very sad.
She dropped everything and ran to her car with shaking hands.
The drive to the hospital felt long but was only fifteen minutes.
When she came, the doctors waited for her.
They told her Ethan was hit by a car after school.
He died quickly and did not feel pain.
The words do not make sense at first.
Did someone die or is it just a question.
Is her son here or not.
No, that is not right.
Ethan was to come home that afternoon but he did not.
He was meant to do his homework but did not.
He was to have dinner with her.
He grows up, goes to college, and has a family.
He was meant to bury her, but it was the other way.
Anna realized the doctors were still talking and asking her questions.
What does she want to do now.
Did she want to see Ethan.
Did she want to pick clothes for him.
The questions feel very hard and make me worried.
How can Anna choose clothes for Ethan if he won't wear them.
How can she think about what he wears when he is gone.
Still, she went home and felt alone.
Anna walked into Ethan's room and saw his drawing on the desk.
It showed a heart and said, "I love you, Mom.
Anna picked it up and cried for the first time.
She opened Ethan's closet and looked at his clothes.
She chose his blue sweater because he wanted to wear it.
She chose the jeans that he likes best.
She chose warm socks so her boy would not be cold.
The thought makes her cry more and feel sad.
He will never feel warm or cold again.
He will never feel anything again.
The days after that were very confusing and unclear.
Friends and family come to visit Anna and Ethan.
They brought food and shared it with everyone.
They brought flowers and gave them to Anna.
They brought words of comfort, but Anna could not hear them.
She moved through each day slowly and felt far away.
She went to the funeral and felt very sad.
She watched them lower her son's small casket into the ground.
She went home to a house that now feels empty.
Weeks go by and time changes.
Then there are months.
Anna went back to work but felt like a new person.
She did her daily tasks, but nothing felt real to her.
Anna wakes up and forgets for a moment, but then remembers Ethan is gone.
One afternoon, Anna cleaned Ethan's room six months after his death.
She put it off but knew she must not leave it forever.
She organizes his books and finds a notebook.
Ethan wrote a list inside the house.
Anna sat on the floor and read a list of kind ideas.
Her son thinks about helping others.
At eight years old, he wanted to make the world better.
And now he will never do it again.
But then Anna had a new idea.
What if Anna can do these things for Ethan.
What if she helps people like Ethan wanted.
The idea felt strange at first, like wearing other clothes.
It felt like a way to honor Ethan's spirit and memory.
Anna begins small and grows bigger.
Anna organized a toy drive and collected toys for sick children.
Anna visited a nursing home and spent time with lonely people.
She made cards for people who felt sad like her.
Each time she helped someone, she felt Ethan's presence with her.
But it was not enough for Anna.
Anna wants to do more things and be happy.
She thought about what Ethan loved the most.
He loved school very much.
He loved learning very much.
He loved helping kids who had problems.
Anna remembered when he came home and talked about classmates needing help.
One day, Anna talked to a friend at a hospital.
The friend said many children there feel alone.
They cannot go to school today.
They cannot see their friends now or later.
They spent weeks in the hospital and felt alone from the world.
Anna thinks about Ethan's list again.
Anna makes cards for sad people and wants to help more.
What if she helps sick children stay close to school and friends.
What if she gives them technology to talk and feel less alone.
The idea grew in Anna's mind and made her happy.
She started to research and learn new things.
She learned about programs that help sick children with technology.
She learned about robots that help children go to school online.
She learned about tablets and computers that connect children to teachers.
Anna wants to start a nonprofit group to help others.
She named it Ethan's Connection after her son.
She started to raise money for a good cause.
She reached out to hospitals for help and support.
She found people who believed in her work and helped her.
The organization grew slowly and became bigger.
Ethan's Connection gives technology to fifty children in one year.
In two years, that number grows to two hundred.
The work was hard, but Anna tried her best.
Anna often feels very overwhelmed.
Anna comes home tired and misses Ethan very much.
There were days when Anna wanted to give up and felt sad.
On a hard day, Anna visits a hospital.
Anna delivers a tablet to Maya, who is in the hospital.
Maya's face lit up when she saw the tablet.
She started a video call with her best friend Anna.
Anna watched Maya laugh and talk, forgetting she was sick.
She was a kid and talked to her friend.
Anna watched and felt something strange.
It was a feeling Anna had not felt for a long time.
It was a feeling of peace, purpose, and connection.
Ethan is with her and tells her this is right.
Anna saw that helping others is about more than Ethan's memory.
It was about Anna finding meaning in her life again.
Anna takes her love for her son and shares it with children.
She changes her pain into purpose and helps others.
The work did not help with the grief.
Anna misses Ethan every day and feels sad.
She still cries sometimes and feels sad.
She still has times when the loss feels very hard.
Now she has joy when she sees a child's happy face.
One evening, Anna was at home and looked at photos of Ethan.
She found a picture of him helping a child at school.
Ethan shows a classmate how to solve a math problem.
His face is serious and kind when he helps others understand.
Anna looked at the photo and thought about the children she helped.
She thinks about Maya, who can now go to school from bed.
She thinks about other children who stay connected and feel less alone.
Anna saw something important and understood it.
Ethan's life was short, but it was important.
She helps others and keeps Ethan's spirit alive.
Anna took Ethan's love and shared it with children in need.
Anna knew she would always feel sad for Ethan.
The loss is always a part of Anna.
She knew she could live with her loss and make it beautiful.
She found her purpose and helped others like Ethan wanted.
Years go by and change many things.
Ethan's Connection grows and becomes very important.
It helped many children and made them happy.
Anna spoke at conferences and shared her ideas.
She met other parents who lost their children too.
She shared her story and they shared their stories too.
She learned she was not alone and helping others helped her heal.
One day, Anna got a letter from a mother about a tablet.
The daughter attends her graduation from her hospital bed with Anna's help.
The mother wrote she will never forget what Anna did.
Anna read the letter and cried, but her tears felt different.
They were not only tears of sadness and loss.
They were tears of love, pride, and gratitude.
She took the worst thing in her life and made it good.
Anna knew Ethan would not come back.
She knew she would always miss him very much.
She knew his love lived on in her work and the children she helped.
Anna changes her grief into purpose and keeps Ethan's spirit alive.
